methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $63M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Bedrock Ocean is a Series A-stage company operating in the ocean and maritime sector. Founded by Anthony DiMare and Charles Chiau, the company has raised $63M to date. The valuation analysis is constrained by the absence of public comparables and identified private peers in this frontier subsector, limiting triangulation to stage-based methodology.

Stage-progression analysis, applicable given the company's Series A status, applies a 2.0x multiplier to the $63M total raised as a value proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $126M. This multiplier reflects typical step-up dynamics between Series A and subsequent financing rounds. The absence of sector-specific or peer company data prevents cross-validation via revenue multiples, precedent transaction analysis, or discounted cash flow methods.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $107M (low) to $145M (high), centered on $126M. The wide 35% range between low and high reflects reliance on a single methodological input and material uncertainty regarding comparable company precedents in ocean technology. Key uncertainties include the depth of Bedrock Ocean's technical differentiation, capital intensity and unit economics of ocean-sector businesses, and the absence of exit precedents. Additional financial metrics—revenue, burn rate, and pathway to profitability—would substantially narrow this range and support more robust valuation work.
